title: SAGE-Kit Ready Resolution Result v1
description: A bounded, language-neutral decision output, not runtime mutation. Result and Error are mutually exclusive outputs: invalid, oversized, or Graph-mismatched input produces Error, not a partial Result or fabricated BLOCKED node. The entire result instance has a deterministic canonical strict JSON limit of 16777216 bytes, measured with the complete canonical_byte_budgets algorithm in contract.json: UTF-8 without BOM, object keys lexicographically sorted by Unicode code point sequence, no insignificant whitespace, shortest decimal integers, fixed necessary JSON string escaping, and all other Unicode scalars encoded directly as UTF-8 rather than ASCII escape. Mandatory representation escaping never rewrites identity. This aggregate measurement is admission only, not semantic identity or authority. An individual opaque identity is never rewritten or truncated. A resolver must not generate an over-limit partial or false-success result; it returns RESULT_TOO_LARGE Error and does not truncate success. The byte budget does not alter the Graph digest and does not shrink the Graph Contract or its identity domain, and applies only to the optional resolver invocation. A Result is valid only for the matching input_digest. An input_digest mismatch makes it non-reusable, and a host must not reuse a Result from graph_digest alone. The binding covers node states, AVAILABLE, BUSY, UNAVAILABLE, and UNKNOWN resource snapshots, and external_join_decision authority and evidence even though satisfied manual or corrective join evidence is not copied into the Result. The result separates opaque blocking node and resource identities from bounded repository-neutral authority and evidence references. It contains dispositions, stable reason codes, typed blocking identities, repository-neutral blocking references, and counts only. It contains no command, callback, process handle, agent packet, mutable object, private reasoning, transcript, environment dump, credential, secret, stdout, stderr, or arbitrary payload. The host decides whether and how to map this result to an authorized event or state update.

schema_id.description: Stable identity of the Ready Resolution Result v1 boundary.

schema_version.description: Version of this language-neutral result boundary.

graph_digest.description: Graph semantic identity copied from input; resource integrity digests are not execution identity.

graph_generation.description: Observed Graph generation copied from input. It grants no Graph mutation authority.

input_digest.description: Domain-separated semantic identity of the complete strictly validated and normalized Resolution Input. The Result is valid only for this exact matching input_digest. It is not authority, permission, acceptance, a Graph digest, or a resource-integrity digest.

graph_disposition.description: Aggregate decision only. WAITING includes resource or dependency waiting without upgrading it to BLOCKED. HANDOFF_REQUIRED is not completion. COMPLETED is not Project Manager acceptance.

node_decisions.description: One bounded disposition per considered Graph node. The contract does not apply transitions.

join_decisions.description: Bounded aggregation/decision outcomes for declared joins, never hidden node dependencies.

summary.description: Bounded non-negative integer counts only; no executable or mutable payload.

node_id.description: Opaque non-empty Graph node semantic identity. Even when its text looks like a path, it is preserved exactly and is never path-normalized, rejected as a path, treated as file access, or used for a filesystem operation.

graph_id.description: Opaque non-empty Graph resource or join semantic identity. Even when its text looks like a path, it is preserved exactly and is never path-normalized, rejected as a path, treated as file access, or used for a filesystem operation.

reference.description: Bounded repository-neutral evidence or authority reference. Absolute paths, UNC or rooted paths, file URIs, HTTP(S) URIs, and newline-bearing values are invalid.

reason_code.description: Stable bounded machine-readable reason. Human-readable prose is not required authority.

node_decision.description: A pure disposition for one node. READY may be computed only from Graph nodes truly present in depends_on. A failed required dependency cannot yield READY, optional classification does not override explicit depends_on, DONE_WITH_CONCERNS does not satisfy required success, and HANDOFF remains HANDOFF_REQUIRED rather than COMPLETED.

node_id.description: Opaque non-empty Graph Contract v1 node identity, preserved exactly without an ASCII grammar or 256-character restriction.

reason_codes.description: Bounded deterministic machine-readable reasons, never unbounded prose.

blocking_node_ids.description: Bounded unique opaque Graph node identities that block this node decision. Values are semantic identities, never references or filesystem paths.

blocking_resource_ids.description: Bounded unique opaque Graph resource identities that block this node decision. Values are semantic identities, never references or filesystem paths.

blocking_refs.description: Bounded unique repository-neutral authority or evidence references associated with the blocker. Opaque node, resource, and join identities never belong in this field.

join_decision.description: A pure disposition for one declared join. A join is an aggregation and decision boundary, not a hidden scheduler edge. Missing manual-gate or corrective-join evidence is REQUIRES_EXTERNAL_DECISION, not node success. Automatic join policy cannot be bypassed by an external decision.

join_id.description: Opaque non-empty Graph Contract v1 join identity, preserved exactly without an ASCII grammar or 256-character restriction.

reason_codes.description: Bounded deterministic machine-readable reasons, never approval prose.

blocking_node_ids.description: Bounded unique opaque Graph node identities that block this join decision. External authority or evidence remains in blocking_refs.

blocking_refs.description: Bounded unique repository-neutral authority or evidence references. Opaque node identities never belong in this field, and references cannot replace node identities for an automatic join failure.
